jokes.text: You're eating a sandwich from a men's room? What's the worst thing that could happen?
jokes.setup: Character eating suspicious food asks what could go wrong
jokes.punchline: Famous last words delivered with naive confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - his oblivious optimism about terrible decisions. Classic dramatic irony with strong payoff potential.

jokes.text: It's like a party in my mouth, and everyone's throwing up.
jokes.setup: Traditional 'party in my mouth' positive food metaphor
jokes.punchline: Twisted into disgusting imagery while maintaining the party metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of familiar phrase with perfect comedic economy. Genuinely creative wordplay that's both gross and clever.

jokes.text: Yosemite Sam?
jokes.setup: Reference to mud flaps with Yosemite Sam silhouette
jokes.punchline: Innocent observation about seeing the cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Simple visual reference joke. Functional but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: Fry, I'd rather not dignify them with an ass-whipping.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to be diplomatic about avoiding confrontation
jokes.punchline: Frames violence as dignifying the opponents rather than concerning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's tough-but-reasonable personality. Well-structured understatement.

jokes.text: Yo, sexy mama, let's get busy and freaky, in that order.
jokes.setup: Crude pickup line
jokes.punchline: Adding unnecessary logical sequence to vulgar proposition
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of pickup line cliche with absurd specificity about order of operations.

jokes.text: You take that back! She does not look good for a truck-stop chick!
jokes.setup: Fry defending Leela's honor
jokes.punchline: Accidentally confirms the insult while trying to defend her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - well-intentioned but completely backwards logic. Great character-driven comedy.

jokes.text: You're right, she don't got enough meat for me.
jokes.setup: Fry's accidental insult gives trucker ammunition
jokes.punchline: Trucker's crude assessment of Leela's body
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that escalates the conflict but relies on basic body shaming for the laugh.

jokes.text: She does too! She's got more meat than a cow.
jokes.setup: Fry continuing to 'defend' Leela
jokes.punchline: Makes it exponentially worse with cow comparison
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the previous joke. Fry digging himself deeper with each attempt to help.

jokes.text: Who are you? / Scruffy. A janitor. / Why aren't you fixing the boiler? / Schedule conflict.
jokes.setup: Emergency situation with critical equipment failure
jokes.punchline: Janitor calmly prioritizes his schedule over disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect introduction to Scruffy's deadpan, bureaucratic personality. Excellent economy and character establishment.

jokes.text: Who are you? Scruffy. A janitor. Why aren't you fixing the boiler? Schedule conflict.
jokes.setup: Emergency situation requires boiler repair
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's matter-of-fact refusal due to vague 'schedule conflict'
jokes.explanation: Perfect introduction to Scruffy's character - deadpan, unhelpful, mysteriously bureaucratic. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Scruffy's gonna die the way he lived.
jokes.setup: Explosion happening around him
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of death with vague non-explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Scruffy line. Mysterious, darkly funny, and completely character-specific. Became a fan favorite quote.

jokes.text: Oh, marmalade.
jokes.setup: Mild exclamation in response to disaster
jokes.punchline: Extremely understated reaction using quaint food item as expletive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy character moment. Absurd understatement that fits his polite, old-fashioned demeanor.

jokes.text: My lead pipe hurts a little.
jokes.setup: Medical complaint about body part
jokes.punchline: References Monopoly game piece instead of actual anatomy
jokes.explanation: Decent Fry confusion moment. Board game reference is mildly clever but not exceptional.

jokes.text: He's a witch!
jokes.setup: Doctor correctly diagnoses problem
jokes.punchline: Fry's medieval explanation for modern medicine
jokes.explanation: Good Fry fish-out-of-water moment. His 20th century brain jumping to superstition.

jokes.text: If you can't see well enough, there'll be a close-up on this video screen.
jokes.setup: Medical examination for privacy
jokes.punchline: Immediately defeats privacy with giant video display
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction joke. Medical privacy immediately undermined by Professor's obliviousness.

jokes.text: Guess again.
jokes.setup: Suggesting camera insertion method
jokes.punchline: Implication of more invasive entry route
jokes.explanation: Relies on crude implication. Economical delivery but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's gorgeous. That place used to be a dump.
jokes.setup: Looking inside Fry's intestines with worms improving it
jokes.punchline: Literal dump (intestines) looking better than before
jokes.explanation: Clever dual meaning - both literal intestinal improvement and 'dump' as slang. Good visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: And the mayonnaise wasn't too fresh either.
jokes.setup: Already established egg salad had worm eggs
jokes.punchline: Casual addition that mayonnaise was also bad
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of disgusting sandwich details. Mild additional gross-out humor.

jokes.text: That would require extremely tiny atoms. Have you priced those lately?
jokes.setup: Question about shrinking technology
jokes.punchline: Treats atomic particles like expensive retail items
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic mixing quantum physics with economic concerns. Very quotable Professor moment.

jokes.text: I'm not made of money. Leave me alone!
jokes.setup: Continuing complaint about atomic particle costs
jokes.punchline: Defensive overreaction to reasonable question
jokes.explanation: Good Professor character moment showing his cranky, defensive nature about scientific expenses.

jokes.text: There's no part of that sentence I didn't like.
jokes.setup: Description of experiencing Fry's worm-infested bowels
jokes.punchline: Bender's enthusiastic endorsement of disgusting experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - his perverse enthusiasm for gross/inappropriate things. Great character specificity.

jokes.text: Like how to make ice-cream soup?
jokes.setup: Discussion of what worms would know from Fry's knowledge
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and useless skill from Fry's repertoire
jokes.explanation: Good character joke about Fry's limited/weird skillset. Absurd but believable for him.

jokes.text: Let's go where he won't put his finger.
jokes.setup: Trying to avoid Fry's finger in his ear
jokes.punchline: Implications about where else finger might go
jokes.explanation: Crude humor relying on anatomical implications. Not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: My God! Soon he'll be smarter than Cher!
jokes.setup: Worms doubling Fry's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Using Cher as benchmark for moderate intelligence
jokes.explanation: Decent celebrity reference joke. Economical but relies on pop culture target rather than original wit.

jokes.text: I learned it from a decongestant commercial: 'Soothing action...'
jokes.setup: Bender unexpectedly knowing anatomy
jokes.punchline: Source is TV commercial rather than education
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment about his TV-based 'knowledge.' Fits his lazy learning style.

jokes.text: Better known as the love muscle. / Which digests food.
jokes.setup: Bender's romantic description of the heart
jokes.punchline: Amy's completely wrong biological function
jokes.explanation: Good two-part joke with Bender's pseudo-romantic knowledge corrected by Amy's worse knowledge.

jokes.text: It's good cholesterol, but it spreads like bad cholesterol.
jokes.setup: Medical observation about cholesterol
jokes.punchline: Contradictory properties of good vs bad cholesterol behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever medical contradiction joke. Good Professor pseudo-scientific observation.

jokes.text: They're Jazzercising Fry's muscles. He'll be as strong and flexible as Gumby and Hercules combined.
jokes.setup: Worms are improving Fry's physical condition
jokes.punchline: Absurd combination of opposite archetypes (flexible cartoon vs strong hero)
jokes.explanation: Funny visual concept and good juxtaposition of opposing character types.

jokes.text: Gum-ercules?! I love that guy.
jokes.setup: Professor's portmanteau of Gumby and Hercules
jokes.punchline: Bender treating made-up character as real celebrity
jokes.explanation: Fun wordplay with Bender's characteristic enthusiasm for pop culture references, even fake ones.

jokes.text: Sorry, ma'am. I've learned a lesson about not ogling cans I won't forget.
jokes.setup: Apology for harassment after being beaten up
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'cans' - containers vs anatomy
jokes.explanation: Decent pun on 'cans' but somewhat crude and predictable. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This is gonna be one hell of a bowel movement.
jokes.setup: Plan to stimulate bowel to expel worms
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of explosive bathroom consequences
jokes.explanation: Straightforward bathroom humor. Professor's clinical approach to crude topic is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: He'll be lucky to have bones left.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of explosive bowel consequences
jokes.punchline: Extreme escalation of physical damage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of previous joke with absurd consequences. Economical but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Is it about Bender? / No, it's about you and me. / And Bender?
jokes.setup: Fry trying to have romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Leela's fixation on including Bender in everything
jokes.explanation: Good running gag about Leela's obsession with including Bender. Well-structured interruption of romantic moment.

jokes.text: Freshen your naugateena?
jokes.setup: Restaurant server offering drink refill
jokes.punchline: Made-up drink name that sounds vaguely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever made-up word that sounds suggestive. Background humor but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I call it Caribbean Drano.
jokes.setup: Reference to Hermes' jerk prunes for digestive issues
jokes.punchline: Comparing laxative to drain cleaner with tropical flair
jokes.explanation: Good Hermes line combining his Caribbean background with crude comparison. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Apartment 1-I. The old me would joke about that.
jokes.setup: Fry showing his improved apartment number
jokes.punchline: Self-awareness about his former immaturity while still making the reference
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke showing character growth while still acknowledging the obvious pun. Well-structured self-awareness.

jokes.text: I'm thinking of having a window installed. / The view's perfect already.
jokes.setup: Fry mentioning home improvement to windowless apartment
jokes.punchline: Smooth romantic line about Leela being the perfect view
jokes.explanation: Classic smooth romantic comeback. Well-structured but fairly conventional pickup line territory.

jokes.text: I haven't felt this happy since double-soup Tuesday.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing happiness about his relationship with Leela
jokes.punchline: His happiness baseline is a mundane cafeteria event
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry line showing his simple pleasures and food obsession. Endearingly pathetic.

jokes.text: I am the lord mayor of Cologne! / You mean colon?
jokes.setup: Fry mispronouncing anatomical location
jokes.punchline: Worm correcting to proper body part term
jokes.explanation: Simple pun on similar sounding words. Economical but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: I thought I was once. But then I remembered we reproduce with spore clouds.
jokes.setup: Asked about being in love
jokes.punchline: Biological reality of worm reproduction negating romance
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic. Scientific reality undermining romantic sentiment in unexpectedly funny way.

jokes.text: My ancestors came here on the sandwich.
jokes.setup: Worm defending his right to stay in Fry's body
jokes.punchline: Parody of immigrant 'came here on the boat' stories
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of immigrant origin stories applied to parasites. Creative, economical, and unexpectedly touching.

jokes.text: Obviously you've never been in love.
jokes.setup: Worm saying he wouldn't make himself an idiot
jokes.punchline: Fry's observation that love makes you do stupid things
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry wisdom moment. His genuine insight about love's irrationality delivered with conviction.

jokes.text: I hope Satan has a nice colon, because that's where you're going.
jokes.setup: Threat about where Fry will go when he dies
jokes.punchline: Combining afterlife punishment with anatomical location
jokes.explanation: Creative threat combining religious concepts with biological reality. Dark humor with good structure.

jokes.text: Ever wonder what makes 'special sauce' so special? Yo.
jokes.setup: Parting threat about future fast food contamination
jokes.punchline: Implication about parasites in restaurant condiments
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor about food contamination. Effective final threat but relies on crude implications.

jokes.text: I don't know. I got nervous and started thinking about neck bolts.
jokes.setup: Failing to play romantic music on holophonor
jokes.punchline: Random mechanical thoughts disrupting performance
jokes.explanation: Good Fry character moment showing his scattered brain under pressure. Absurd but believable distraction.

jokes.text: Wow, did you see that vase break? It was all like:
jokes.setup: Fry nervously commenting on accident
jokes.punchline: Attempting to recreate sound effect of breaking
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment showing Fry's awkwardness. Character-appropriate but not exceptionally clever.

jokes.text: I'll give you a back rub just like I used to give Amy. She always seemed to... Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Fry offering romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing previous relationship details
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Fry's tactless reference to ex during romantic moment. Character-driven awkwardness.

jokes.text: Babes, bucks, I got it all.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming his life is great
jokes.punchline: Bragging about shallow materialistic success
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender boasting. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: I gotta get up early to go parasailing with movie stars.
jokes.setup: Continuing Bender's boasts about his lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and unlikely activity with celebrities
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Bender's lifestyle claims. Character-appropriate exaggeration with specific details.
